ps140: O Titus, our divinely wise father,* bearing your cross you followed after Christ* and made all the passions subject to your soul;* wherefore, you have received the grace from on high* to heal the sufferings and put down the afflictions of those who have recourse to you,* and to cast out evil spirits.* Therefore, we celebrate and bless your memory.

O Titus, our most blessed father,* having learned sacred things by ascetic living,* and illumined your mind by divine communion,* at the behest of the divine Spirit* you truly received the most holy anointing of the priesthood,* and, like an angel, you served God your Master well* upon the earth.

O venerable and divinely inspired Titus,* illumining your soul with the light of the Orthodox Faith,* you diminished the gloomy darkness of heresy;* and, shining like a most splendid star,* you ever enlighten the ends of the world with your luminous miracles.* Therefore, holding festival, we celebrate your memory.
theotokion: My thoughts are impure, my lips false, and all my works defiled.* What, then, shall I do?* How can I meet the Judge?* O Virgin Mistress, entreat the Lord, your Son and Creator,* that He accept my soul in repentance,* in that He alone is compassionate.
stavrotheotokion: The unblemished heifer,* beholding her Calf nailed of His own will to the Tree,* cried out, lamenting piteously:* Woe is me, O my Child most beloved!* How have the thankless assembly of the Jews rewarded You,* desiring to leave me bereft of You,* my Child most beloved?
